about.title: About Me
about.content: Fullstack Developer with a diploma in Full Stack Software Development from Code Institute. My experience as a gaming coach has sharpened my problem-solving skills and ability to adapt to different challenges. I build responsive, user-friendly web applications and I have an eye for details. I value clarity and efficiency in my work and enjoy creating solutions that work seamlessly for users.

education.title: 2024 Full Stack Software Development Diploma
education.content: Code Institute. Full-stack comprehensive course covering HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Python, and Django. Advanced full-stack development with React. The program gave me the skills to build dynamic, responsive web applications from scratch and integrate frontend and backend technologies into complete solutions.

education.title: 2023 Self-Taught Developer
education.content: Developed foundational full-stack skills through self-directed learning with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and Python. Built a habit of continuous learning and adaptability that prepared me for structured training at Code Institute.

experience.title: 2024 AI Trainer and Senior Reviewer at Outlier (Scale AI)
experience.content: Worked on multiple RLHF projects as a Senior Reviewer. Led side-by-side evaluations, rubric tuning, and final quality checks. Reviewed prompts, rewrites, and model outputs to improve accuracy, clarity, tone, and safety. Mentored reviewers and flagged edge cases to refine guidelines.

experience.title: 2023 Development Coach at AimLab.gg
experience.content: Created personalized coaching content by video. Answered client questions and delivered actionable feedback. Helped build a supportive learning community and improved engagement through clear communication.

experience.title: 2019 Coach & Talent Coordinator at ProGuides
experience.content: Delivered one-on-one coaching for Valorant and Fortnite to more than 2000 clients. Wrote engaging video scripts that combined expert tips with storytelling for an audience of over one million subscribers.

projects.title: Role Ready
projects.description: Role Ready is an AI-powered job preparation tool that helps users understand job listings before applying. By pasting a job ad, users get structured insights into the role, the company, and likely interview questions, making it easier to prepare and practice in a focused way. The project is built with a strong emphasis on clean architecture, modern frontend patterns, and scalable auth and layout design.
projects.role: Fullstack product builder focused on frontend architecture, UX, auth flow, and AI-powered experience design.
projects.problem: Job listings are often dense and vague, which makes it harder for applicants to understand the role quickly and prepare with confidence.
projects.solution: Role Ready turns a pasted job listing into structured role insights, company context, and likely interview questions so users can prepare in a more focused way.
projects.impact: Shows Johnny's ability to combine product thinking, modern frontend patterns, auth-aware architecture, and OpenAI integration in a polished real-world app.
keyFeatures: Structured AI breakdown of job ads
Interview prep guidance from listing context
Clean modern UI built for clarity
challenges: Keeping AI output useful instead of generic
Balancing strong UX with scalable architecture
Designing a flow that feels fast and practical for job seekers
projects.whyItMatters: This is one of the strongest examples of Johnny building a practical AI-assisted product with both frontend polish and real user value.

projects.title: Tic Tac Tactics
projects.description: Valorant Tic-Tac-Tactics combines the strategy of Valorant with the simplicity of Tic-Tac-Toe. Built as a fun, lightweight web game for both Valorant fans and casual players.
projects.role: Frontend developer and designer for a lightweight browser game with a strong thematic identity.
projects.problem: Many small browser games are mechanically simple but lack a memorable theme or personality that makes people want to share them.
projects.solution: Johnny combined familiar Tic-Tac-Toe gameplay with Valorant-inspired styling and positioning to create a more playful and recognizable experience.
projects.impact: Demonstrates Johnny's ability to make simple mechanics feel more engaging through theme, presentation, and clean UI execution.
challenges: Making a very simple game feel distinctive
Balancing playful styling with clear usability
projects.whyItMatters: It is a good example of Johnny using design instincts and frontend fundamentals to create a fun, polished small-scope product.

projects.title: Star Review
projects.description: Peer review platform for developers to collaborate, give feedback, and improve code quality. Suitable for both beginners and experienced devs.
projects.role: Frontend-focused fullstack contributor on a peer review platform built around developer collaboration.
projects.problem: Developers often need a simple way to exchange code feedback, especially when they are learning and want peer input without a heavy workflow.
projects.solution: Star Review provides a dedicated platform for peer reviews, collaboration, and code-quality feedback in a more approachable format.
projects.impact: Highlights Johnny's ability to work across frontend and backend concerns in a collaboration-focused product space.
keyFeatures: Fullstack integration with Django REST and PostgreSQL
challenges: Connecting frontend usability with backend review data
Keeping the review experience approachable for a wide range of users
projects.whyItMatters: This project gives recruiters a clear example of Johnny working on a more application-like product instead of a simple static site.

projects.title: Coach Profile
projects.description: A vibrant gaming profile that showcases coaching background, skills, and experiences.
projects.role: Solo frontend builder translating gaming identity and coaching experience into a themed portfolio site.
projects.problem: A standard personal site would not fully reflect Johnny's background in gaming, coaching, and community-driven communication.
projects.solution: Johnny built a themed profile site that blended personality, visual identity, and portfolio-style content into one focused experience.
projects.impact: Shows where Johnny's portfolio work started and how he learned to turn personal identity into a more memorable UI direction.
challenges: Balancing theme with readability
Turning a personality-driven concept into a coherent web experience
projects.whyItMatters: This project reflects the bridge between Johnny's coaching background and the more advanced portfolio work he builds now.

projects.title: Snake
projects.description: Classic Snake implemented in Python and rendered in a mock terminal on Heroku.
projects.role: Builder of a Python-based terminal game adapted for deployment and presentation on the web.
projects.problem: Classic programming projects often stay local and feel less approachable when shown to users or recruiters.
projects.solution: Johnny implemented Snake in Python and presented it in a web-hosted mock terminal format to make the project easier to experience.
projects.impact: Demonstrates backend and logic fundamentals, along with the ability to package a terminal-style experience for a broader audience.
challenges: Presenting a terminal game in a user-friendly way
Maintaining the charm of a command-line experience in a hosted format
projects.whyItMatters: It is a useful example of Johnny's early Python problem-solving and how he thinks about making technical work more accessible.

projects.title: eBuddies
projects.description: Community platform to connect gamers with shared interests. Focus on a welcoming space, sharing experiences, and building connections.
projects.role: Fullstack builder of a community-oriented platform centered on connection and shared interests.
projects.problem: Gamers often need social spaces that feel more welcoming and interest-driven than generic discussion platforms.
projects.solution: eBuddies creates a community platform designed around shared hobbies, interaction, and a friendlier sense of belonging.
projects.impact: Shows Johnny working on community UX, social-product thinking, and backend-backed functionality in a larger fullstack project.
keyFeatures: Fullstack implementation with Django and PostgreSQL
challenges: Designing for community and clarity at the same time
Building an experience that feels welcoming instead of generic
projects.whyItMatters: It is one of the clearer examples of Johnny building for community needs, not just technical showcase value.

projects.title: Portfolio
projects.description: Personal portfolio that showcases projects, skills, and experience. Serves as a digital resume and demo space.
projects.role: Designer and developer of the full portfolio experience, including frontend interaction patterns, assistant features, and content architecture.
projects.problem: A portfolio needs to do more than list projects. It should communicate personality, technical skill, and decision-making in a memorable way.
projects.solution: Johnny built a themed React portfolio with AI chat, voice assistant support, achievement tracking, settings controls, and structured shared content.
projects.impact: Acts as both a resume and a live product demo, showing frontend polish, API integration, accessibility awareness, and iterative product thinking.
keyFeatures: Route-aware chat suggestions and CTA buttons
challenges: Balancing personality-heavy design with maintainable code
Integrating AI features without losing usability
Keeping shared content synchronized between frontend and backend
projects.whyItMatters: This is the project that best combines Johnny's design sense, frontend engineering, AI integration, and product iteration.

hackathons.title: All-inclusive
hackathons.roleSummary: Frontend developer and designer. Built an accessible, intuitive UI aligned with the project's mission of inclusivity by design.
hackathons.projectSummary: All-Inclusive is an accessibility-focused platform built to promote inclusive design and make learning resources easier to use for a wider range of people.
hackathons.description: Frontend developer and designer. Built an accessible, intuitive UI. The project promotes inclusivity by design and offers accessibility-focused education.
hackathons.role: Frontend developer and designer focused on accessibility-first UI decisions.
hackathons.problem: Accessibility is often treated as an afterthought, which leaves many users with harder or less inclusive product experiences.
hackathons.solution: The team created an accessibility-focused platform that promotes inclusive design and makes educational content easier to navigate.
hackathons.impact: Shows Johnny contributing thoughtful frontend and UX work in a mission-driven team project with a clear social purpose.
keyFeatures: Clearer UX for broader user needs
challenges: Translating accessibility goals into actual interface choices
Designing quickly while still keeping usability standards high
hackathons.whyItMatters: This is one of the strongest hackathon examples of Johnny connecting design work with real user inclusion goals.

hackathons.title: 12 Days of Quizmas
hackathons.roleSummary: Frontend developer and UX designer. Designed and implemented the UI to create a playful and intuitive quiz experience.
hackathons.projectSummary: A holiday quiz game built around Christmas traditions, with multiple-choice questions and a lightweight festive interface.
hackathons.description: Frontend dev and UX designer. Designed and implemented the UI for a quiz game about Christmas traditions.
hackathons.role: Frontend developer and UX designer shaping the visual direction and user flow.
hackathons.problem: Short hackathon projects can feel rushed or generic if the user experience is not prioritized early.
hackathons.solution: Johnny helped build a playful festive quiz experience with an interface that was easy to understand and visually inviting.
hackathons.impact: Demonstrates quick UX decision-making and the ability to make a time-boxed project feel polished and cohesive.
challenges: Designing a complete user flow under time pressure
Keeping the interface playful without hurting clarity
hackathons.whyItMatters: It is a good example of Johnny bringing UX structure and visual polish to a fast-moving team build.

hackathons.title: Jedi Blades
hackathons.roleSummary: Fullstack developer and co-scrum master. Helped bring the product vision to life with a strong frontend and collaboration focus.
hackathons.projectSummary: A Star Wars themed encyclopedia where users explore lightsabers, their lore, and the characters who wield them.
hackathons.description: Fullstack developer and co-scrum master. Built a Star Wars themed encyclopedia where users explore lightsabers and their wielders.
hackathons.role: Fullstack developer and co-scrum master with a strong frontend and collaboration focus.
hackathons.problem: Hackathon teams need quick coordination and a product concept that is broad enough to divide work while still feeling cohesive.
hackathons.solution: The team built a Star Wars themed encyclopedia around lightsabers and their wielders, with Johnny contributing across the stack and helping coordinate delivery.
hackathons.impact: Highlights both technical contribution and leadership qualities in a collaborative, fast-paced build environment.
challenges: Balancing team coordination with hands-on development
Keeping the product coherent while multiple contributors moved quickly
hackathons.whyItMatters: This is one of the clearest hackathon examples of Johnny combining implementation with team leadership.

hackathons.title: Fund Frontier
hackathons.roleSummary: Built the UI for an NPV calculator and also contributed to the README and product flow.
hackathons.projectSummary: A finance tool that compares the Net Present Value of up to three projects to support clearer investment decisions.
hackathons.description: Created the UI for a Net Present Value calculator to compare up to three projects. Helped with README and flow design.
hackathons.role: Frontend-focused contributor on interface design, product flow, and documentation.
hackathons.problem: Finance tools can be intimidating if the interface does not help users understand the comparison clearly.
hackathons.solution: Fund Frontier turned NPV comparison into a more approachable UI that lets users evaluate multiple projects side by side.
hackathons.impact: Shows Johnny adapting quickly to a more finance-oriented product domain and contributing beyond code through flow and README work.
keyFeatures: NPV comparison for up to three projects
challenges: Making a finance concept feel understandable in a short timeframe
Aligning interface choices with a more analytical use case
hackathons.whyItMatters: It is a useful example of Johnny stepping into a less familiar domain and still producing clear, user-focused UI work.

hackathons.title: RemindiCare
hackathons.roleSummary: Led frontend and UX, using Figma, wireframes, and user testing to shape a clearer and more accessible experience.
hackathons.projectSummary: A medication reminder and tracker that helps patients stay on schedule and manage doses more confidently.
hackathons.description: Led frontend and UX. Used Figma, wireframes, and user testing. The app helps patients track medications and get reminders.
hackathons.role: Frontend and UX lead responsible for the product experience from wireframes through testing.
hackathons.problem: Medication adherence is a high-stakes problem, so reminder tools need to feel clear, trustworthy, and easy to use.
hackathons.solution: RemindiCare helps patients track medications and reminders through a more accessible, user-tested interface.
hackathons.impact: This is one of Johnny's strongest examples of combining UX process, health-focused clarity, and frontend execution in a team setting.
keyFeatures: UX process supported by Figma and wireframes
challenges: Designing for a health-related use case where usability matters more
Balancing speed with trust and accessibility
hackathons.whyItMatters: It highlights Johnny's strength in thoughtful UX leadership and frontend delivery on a meaningful user problem.

hackathons.title: Cupid's Quill
hackathons.roleSummary: Worked across the stack with a focus on landing-page animation and the About page experience.
hackathons.projectSummary: A web app that generates gift ideas from personality-based inputs to help users find more thoughtful presents.
hackathons.description: Worked across the stack, focused on landing page animation and the About page. Generates gift ideas from personality inputs.
hackathons.role: Cross-functional contributor working across the stack with emphasis on frontend interaction and page experience.
hackathons.problem: Gift idea tools can feel generic if they do not create a playful or emotionally engaging entry point for users.
hackathons.solution: The team built a personality-based gift idea generator, while Johnny focused on the landing page feel and About page presentation.
hackathons.impact: Shows Johnny contributing both technically and experientially, especially in the parts of the product that shape first impressions.
keyFeatures: Gift ideas generated from personality-based inputs
challenges: Creating a playful tone without making the experience feel messy
Contributing across multiple areas within hackathon constraints
hackathons.whyItMatters: It reflects Johnny's flexibility in team projects and his habit of improving the parts of a product users notice first.
